videos.title: Behind The Scenes With GCN - Alex’s Australian Adventure
videos.description:
Alex went to Australia with just one day’s notice to film some content ahead of the Tour Down Under. While he was there he decided to vlog his trip to the country, to show what life is like behind the scenes for a GCN presenter on an epic overseas adventure. From checking out the bikes of the WorldTour to going searching for kangaroos, this is what he got up to in Adelaide!

00:00 Intro
02:00 Day 1
03:55 Day 2
05:57 Day 3
09:08 Day 4
11:03 Day 5
12:41 Day 6

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside Cycling

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person vlog from GCN presenter documenting the Tour Down Under experience with good production quality and high view count.

videos.title: South African inked into  road-cycling history books
videos.description:
A South African name has been inked into the history books of global road-cycling.

Daryl Impey is a two-time defending champion of the Tour Down Under.

The race in Australia is the first event of the elite UCI World Tour season that includes the likes of the Tour de France and the Vuelta.

Now based in Europe, Impey is in SA for a few weeks.

eNCA Sports Reporter Hloni Mtimkulu caught up with him.

 #DStv403

weeks he joins us now Darrell tell us
just your plans and why you are here

videos.title: Thousands take part in Amy's ride
videos.description: Thousands of cyclists have taken part in a charity ride from Adelaide to McLaren Vale in memory of cyclist Amy Gillett.
videos.transcript:
thousands of cyclists have hit the roads
of the Southern suburbs in a show of
strength to promote Road Safety they
were taking part in a charity event in
memory of cyclist Amy
gillet from the start at Flinders
University waves of lro clad Riders wo
their way down the Southern Expressway
it's the fourth annual event remembering
South Australian cyclist Amy gillet who
died after being hit by a car while

videos.title: 2017 Santos Tour Down Under - Stage 2 Preview, Stirling to Paracombe
videos.description: Tour Down Under winner, Patrick Jonker, and Australian University Games Cycling Gold Medalist for UniSA, Madeleine Steele, preview Stage 2 of the 2017 Santos Tour Down Under from Stirling to Paracombe.

key_challenges.name: Southern Expressway section
key_challenges.description: Part of the route that follows the Southern Expressway from Flinders University toward McLaren Vale, with riders noting the 100km journey takes its toll with unexpected pit stops needed along the way.

additional_quotes.text: For the 100 kilm Riders the journey took its toll with a few unexpected pit stops along the way
About 3,000 people on Two wheed And three-wheed bikes pedal their way to McLaren Vil choosing to ride between 25 and 100 kilomet
From the start at Flinders University waves of lro clad Riders wo their way down the Southern Expressway

rider_intel.search_text: The Tour Down Under Community Ride is a charity cycling event in Adelaide's southern suburbs that attracts approximately 3,000 participants on two and three-wheeled bikes. The route starts at Flinders University and follows the Southern Expressway toward McLaren Vale, with riders choosing between 25km and 100km distance options. The longer 100km route proves challenging, with riders experiencing fatigue and requiring unexpected pit stops along the way. The event serves as both a memorial ride for cyclist Amy Gillet and a road safety awareness initiative. Riders wear lycra and participate in waves, creating a significant presence on Adelaide's roads. The community aspect is strong, with the event aiming to raise over $100,000 for road safety initiatives while promoting safe cycling practices and educating both motorists and cyclists about road sharing.
